message:
perf(vdbe): promote IdxRowid into try_execute_hot_opcode hot-path

Mirrors the just-promoted Rowid arm: IdxRowid's body routes through
the same `cursor_rowid` helper (HashMap probe + cursor.rowid call +
register write), so the hot-path arm is byte-identical to the
main-match arm.  Adds `test_idx_rowid_via_hot_path` and a
`vdbe_pipeline_execute_idx_rowid` bench arm following the existing
Rowid layout (no Init, halt label resolved at the program-end Halt
so Rewind p2 satisfies the strictly-`< op_count` verifier rule).

Win sizes (criterion, p<0.05, warm-up 1s, measure 4s):

| variant | before  | after   | Δ thrpt |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| /64    | 3.42 µs | 3.24 µs | +5.3%  |
| /256   | 9.67 µs | 8.98 µs | +7.6%  |
| /1024  | 34.82 µs| 32.03 µs| +8.7%  |

Same magnitude as the Rowid promotion (+2.4 / +4.6 / +5.4% conservative,
~9-10% on cross-check) — both opcodes share the same `cursor_rowid`
body, so the dispatch-window shave is identical in shape.

~13 production codegen sites in fsqlite-vdbe + fsqlite-planner: every
index-driven JOIN inner loop, IN-list lookup, covering-index scan
that maps index entries to table rowids for SeekRowid, and any
DELETE/UPDATE walking an index for row keys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
